steps.title: Initial Setup/Scanning
steps.instruction: Compile comprehensive list of all discovered access points and entry vectors
steps.tip: Start with the most reliable information source for attack path mapping framework
steps.expected_output: Documented list of all potential attack vectors with corresponding services

steps.title: Data Collection/Configuration
steps.instruction: For each access point, identify logical next steps for privilege escalation or lateral movement
steps.tip: Organize data systematically to avoid duplicates and ensure coverage
steps.expected_output: Annotated list showing progression possibilities from each access point

steps.title: Analysis/Enumeration
steps.instruction: Create visual diagram showing connections between access points and possible progressions
steps.tip: Look for patterns and correlations in collected data
steps.expected_output: Attack path diagram with nodes representing systems and edges showing progression paths

steps.title: Advanced Technique/Filtering
steps.instruction: Identify the shortest path to reach the defined engagement objective
steps.tip: Use multiple techniques to increase coverage and accuracy
steps.expected_output: Documented shortest attack path with minimal hops to target goal

steps.title: Correlation/Testing
steps.instruction: Document all alternative paths that could reach the same objective
steps.tip: Validate findings across different tools and methods
steps.expected_output: List of alternative attack paths with varying complexity levels

steps.title: Validation/Exploitation
steps.instruction: Rate each path by difficulty, detection risk, and operational impact
steps.expected_output: Scoring matrix ranking paths by risk factors and complexity

steps.title: Documentation/Reporting
steps.instruction: Select and document the top 3 paths with detailed justifications for each
steps.tip: Review all findings before compilation for accuracy and completeness
steps.expected_output: Final report with 3 prioritized attack paths and comprehensive explanations
